methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $7.0B - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: OpenAI

private.name: Anthropic
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $183B

private.name: Anysphere

data.narrative:
Alpha School is a self-funded artificial intelligence and software company at the private stage, founded by MacKenzie Price and Brian Holtz, with a ~$1B Liemandt commitment. The comparable universe consists of nine private frontier-tech firms and one public company (PLTR), providing limited but directionally useful reference points for valuation.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The analysis examined five same-sector, same-stage comparables: OpenAI ($300.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), Anysphere ($29.3B), Neuralink ($9.7B), and Groq ($7.0B). The median valuation across this set is $29.3B, with a range spanning $7.0B to $300.0B. This wide dispersion reflects substantial heterogeneity in investor expectations and technology maturity within frontier AI.

The triangulated valuation range is $24.9B to $33.7B, centered on the peer median of $29.3B. Material uncertainties remain: the peer set exhibits extreme variance, suggesting that comparables provide a point estimate with low confidence; public market data (PLTR) offers limited relevance to a pre-revenue or early-revenue frontier model; and Alpha School's specific IP, team composition, and commercialization pathway remain unmeasured against peer benchmarks. This analysis should be updated as operational metrics (customer traction, unit economics) become available.
